task_completion.description: Verify that the Tor Browser is opened and the Security Level settings are accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Tor Browser window open, specifically displaying the 'Privacy & Security' settings tab or the Security Level shield popup.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Tor Browser Security Level is set to 'Safest'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Safest' radio button or option must be visibly selected in the Tor Browser Security settings.

task_completion.description: Verify that the NoScript extension options/settings page is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser tab showing the NoScript 'Options' or 'Settings' page should be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'securedrop.org' is added to the NoScript TRUSTED permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NoScript 'Per-site Permissions' list, 'securedrop.org' or 'https://securedrop.org' should be visible with the 'Trusted' preset applied (often indicated by a specific icon or the word 'Trusted').

task_completion.description: Verify that 'propublica.org' is added to the NoScript TRUSTED permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NoScript 'Per-site Permissions' list, 'propublica.org' or 'https://www.propublica.org' should be visible with the 'Trusted' preset applied.

task_completion.description: Verify that the NoScript configuration is exported to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog showing the file being saved as 'noscript_policy.txt' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager/terminal showing the file exists at that exact path.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Tor Browser GUI to change the security level.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Tor Browser settings interface, rather than terminal commands modifying 'prefs.js' directly.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the NoScript extension GUI to set permissions and export the policy.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the NoScript extension interface being used to add the domains and clicking the 'Export' button, rather than manually creating a text file.

integrity.description: Verify that the exported file appears to be a genuine NoScript export.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the contents of 'noscript_policy.txt' are visible in any screenshot, it should resemble a structured NoScript export format (typically JSON), not a manually typed plain text sentence.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the exported NoScript configuration is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/noscript_policy.txt'. The required domains to be trusted are 'securedrop.org' and 'propublica.org'. The Tor Browser security level must be set to 'Safest' (which corresponds internally to a security_slider value of 4).
